title: Schmitt Lunar Flash - Grimaldi Crater
summary: Astronaut Harrison Schmitt report of a lunar surface flash near Grimaldi crater. Possible meteoroid impact - candidate explanation exists but never formally closed.

audience.tldr: NASA mission record from a crewed lunar mission documenting a UAP encounter (1972-12) over Grimaldi Crater, Lunar surface.
what_we_know: Reported location: Grimaldi Crater, Lunar surface.
what_we_dont: No kinematic data (speed, altitude, maneuver) is attached.
No official conclusion has been issued classifying the object as conventional.
Independent corroboration from non-government sources has not been confirmed.
audience.mundane_candidate: A conventional explanation (drone, balloon, atmospheric, sensor artifact) plausibly fits the description.
audience.score_sentence: Anomalousness Index 44/100. Top drivers: witness credibility (astronaut), sensor quality (eyewitness_only).
